cross_model.reasoning: Step 1: User wants an elegant outfit for a friend's wedding, ideally burgundy and not too tight. Step 2 (Set A - CLIP): Items include several dresses and skirts in biscuit, dark mink, red, wine, black and aubergine. Visually the set contains a mix of casual and evening pieces but several are short/tight (short dress with puff sleeves, velvet strappy mini, short balloon dress). There is one wine/burgundy top and a red midi with volume, but few true burgundy elegant options and several items contradict the "nothing too tight" constraint. Step 3 (Set B - FashionCLIP): Items include lace midi, draped velvet midi, chiffon godet midi, satin halter (burgundy), leather/garnet top and a long lace/velvet dress in deep brown/burgundy tones. Visually this set offers several formal textures (lace, velvet, satin) and a genuine burgundy satin halter plus long/elegant options that suit a wedding. Step 4 (Relevance to constraints): FashionCLIP matches the burgundy preference and elegance better and contains more non-bodycon, dressier silhouettes. CLIP returns more wrong/neutral colors and multiple short/tight pieces that conflict with the "not too tight" request. Step 5 (Occasion appropriateness): FashionCLIP conveys wedding-appropriate formality (satin, lace, velvet gowns/midis). CLIP is mixed with casual and mini/tight styles less suitable for an elegant wedding look. Step 6 (Visual diversity): FashionCLIP offers meaningful variety in fabric and silhouette for an elegant occasion (midi gowns, lace, satin, velvet). CLIP’s variety leans toward casual skirts/minis and repeats shorter shapes. Step 7 (Overall intent): FashionCLIP better reflects the user’s explicit intent (elegant + burgundy + not too tight). Step 8 (Scores): CLIP overall quality is low–several irrelevant or too-tight items. FashionCLIP is above average–majority are suitable and visually appropriate. Final judgment: FashionCLIP is the stronger set for this query.

set_answer_relevance.reasoning:
Step 1: User wants an elegant wedding outfit, preferably a burgundy dress and not too tight.
Step 2: Reviewed metadata and images: majority items are dresses (mostly brown/dark tones) plus several tops/mini pieces. Only a few items are explicitly burgundy/wine tones (satin halter top, polyamide blend top, a garnet leather mini top, and one aubergine short dress). Many dresses shown are long/flowy but in brown/dark mink rather than burgundy; several items are short or bodycon/minimal coverage (leather mini top, short balloon dress, fringed mini) which are tighter/revealing.
Step 3: Coverage: the set includes multiple dress options but few true burgundy choices and several tops rather than complete elegant dress options. Price points are mid-range and consistent.
Step 4: Visual/style diversity: moderate — there are lace, velvet, satin, chiffon and fringe textures, and lengths vary (midi, mini, maxi). However many pieces share dark brown/wine family tones and several are short/tight.
Step 5: Coherence for occasion: mixed. Some long velvet/satin/chiffon dresses could work for a wedding, but many mini or leather pieces are too casual or revealing for an elegant wedding look.
Step 6: Glaring omissions: a true burgundy, elegant, not-tight midi or maxi dress (flowy, modest silhouette) is missing; few modest, non-bodycon burgundy options.
Step 7: Synthesis: The set contains some potentially elegant pieces but does not prioritize the user’s explicit requests (burgundy color and non-tight fit). Several items conflict with the ‘nothing too tight’ guideline.
Overall judgment: partially relevant but misses key user preferences.

cross_model.reasoning:
Step 1: Query = wedding guest outfit, preferably a midi dress in dusty pink under 100 EUR.
Step 2 (Set A / CLIP): Items are mixed — a short lace slip (pale pink with dark lace) (likely too short for a midi preference/occasion), wide pink trousers (not a dress), a satin belted pink dress (SOSHIOTSUKI) but price 129 EUR (over budget), a biscuit-coloured midi dress (not dusty pink) and an ecru skirt. Visually some pink pieces appear but either wrong silhouette (trousers/short slip) or over the budget/incorrect shade.
Step 3 (Set B / FashionCLIP): Most items are dresses and several are midi-length styles (ruffled midi, trench-style midi, midi with belt, chiffon/lace midi, draped velvet midi). Many items fall under 100 EUR in the metadata and the set offers multiple midi silhouettes. Colours are mostly neutrals and browns with fewer exact dusty-pink examples, but overall more true-to-request midi dresses and within budget.
Step 4: Hard constraints: CLIP fails on category (includes trousers, short dress) and has the key pink satin dress over 100. FashionCLIP better satisfies category (midi dresses) and price constraints (majority under 100). Neither set gives many clear “dusty pink” midi options — CLIP has pink color but wrong length/price; FashionCLIP has correct length and budget but fewer dusty-pink pieces.
Step 5 (occasion/formality): FashionCLIP items present more wedding-appropriate midi silhouettes and fabric choices (chiffon, drape, lace, belted midis). CLIP contains less occasion-appropriate pieces (casual trousers, very short slip, overpriced satin).
Step 6 (visual diversity): FashionCLIP offers a wider variety of different midi dress styles (ruffled, belted, chiffon, lace, velvet) appropriate for guest dressing. CLIP is less diverse in relevant options — more irrelevant categories.
Step 7 (overall intent): The user asked for a midi dusty-pink dress under 100. FashionCLIP better reflects the intent by prioritizing midi dresses and budget items even if the exact dusty-pink shade is limited. CLIP returns some pink items but fails on length/category/budget.
Step 8 (scores): CLIP = 2 (below average — minority of items meet needs). FashionCLIP = 4 (above average — majority of items suitable and visually appropriate for a wedding guest).
Step 9 (winner): fashion_clip — overall more relevant to the user’s request (midi dresses, under/near budget, wedding-appropriate silhouettes).
